ai_insights.executive_summary: CCBD closed February 2026 with a network Performance Score average of 36.4 out of 100 (where 50 = network average), reflecting continued operational strain across five clinics. While month-over-month gains appeared in delay reduction and scheduler compliance at Arlington and MCO, persistent underutilization at three sites and critical overbooking pressure at Huguley signal that gains remain fragile without structural intervention.
highlights: Arlington demonstrated tangible progress, with average patient delay falling from 15.8 minutes in January to 11.8 minutes in February—a 25.0% improvement that now sits 6.5 minutes below the company average of 18.28 minutes. MCO showed equally meaningful movement on scheduler compliance, jumping from 52.5% in January to 68.9% in February, a 31.4% gain that nearly matches the company average of 71.99%, alongside a doubling of iAssign utilization from 4.2% to 8.3% over the same period.
concerns: Plano's average delay of 30.8 minutes represents the most significant performance gap in the network, standing 68% above the company average of 18.28 minutes despite strong scheduler compliance of 85.0%, suggesting that appointment spacing discipline alone is not resolving patient flow friction at that site. We also noticed Weatherford's minutes past closing per patient showing a three-month declining trend (averaging 37.4 minutes), pointing to systemic closure-time pressures that warrant review of daily capacity allocation and treatment sequencing.
recommendations: We recommend piloting a scheduling template audit at Plano focused on chair-to-nurse handoff cadence and appointment interval sizing; the clinic's high compliance score (85.0%) indicates staff follow process, but current intervals may be too ambitious for actual treatment throughput. This targeted intervention—rather than broad process review—will help us understand whether the delay stems from template design, staff capacity constraints, or external patient arrival patterns, and the results will directly inform our approach to similar concerns at Weatherford.

ai_insights.executive_summary: CCBD delivered mixed March results, with the network scoring 36.4 out of 100 on composite performance (where 50 represents the network average). Three clinics showed meaningful month-over-month gains in scheduler compliance and operational delays, while two clinics remain statistical outliers requiring focused attention: Arlington's scheduler compliance at 64.8% versus the company average of 82.7%, and Plano's average patient delay of 28.9 minutes versus the company average of 19.13 minutes.
highlights: Weatherford achieved scheduler compliance of 89.8% in March, up from 81.3% in February—a 10.5 percentage point improvement—and now sits 7.1 points above the company average of 82.7%. MCO's scheduler compliance rose from 68.9% in February to 85.2% in March, a 23.6 percentage point jump that brought the clinic into alignment with network performance, while simultaneously nurse utilization climbed from 33.6% in February to 47.6% in March, reflecting better capacity deployment alongside improved scheduling discipline.
concerns: We noticed Arlington's chair utilization has declined over two consecutive months to 50.3% in March (company average: 73.65%), suggesting structural or scheduling friction worth exploring together—this may be connected to Arlington's scheduler compliance of 64.8%, which sits 1.8 standard deviations below the group mean. Huguley presents a dual picture: strong chair utilization at 133.4% indicates efficient patient flow, but this sits alongside 4.5 treatments running past closing daily (company average: 2.0 treatments), and while treatments past close have improved from 5.8 in February to 4.5 in March, the clinic continues to operate as a statistical outlier 1.8 standard deviations above the group mean.
recommendations: Plano's average patient delay stands at 28.9 minutes, representing a 51% gap above the company average of 19.13 minutes—the largest performance gap across the network. We recommend reviewing Plano's iOptimize scheduling templates and auditing the nurse-to-chair assignment workflow to identify bottlenecks in patient throughput, especially since Plano's iAssign utilization is exceptionally high at 90.9% but delays remain elevated; this suggests the scheduling logic itself may need recalibration rather than increased adoption.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
